zh.lifeSummary: 當Edmund Bacon於大約1289年出生在英國諾福爾可巴根斯索普時，他的父親Adam Bacon21歲，母親Margery Felton19歲。他於1311年與Joan Beaumont在英國奧可福爾結婚。他們至少是1個女兒的父母。他在英國英格蘭奧可福爾瓦林福德被列為constable of wallingford castle。他去世於1336年3月25日在英國英格蘭奧可福爾瓦林福德享年48歲。

zh-hans.lifeSummary: 当Edmund Bacon于大约1289年出生在英国諾福爾可巴根斯索普时，他的父亲Adam Bacon21岁，母亲Margery Felton19岁。他于1311年与Joan Beaumont在英国奧可福爾结婚。他们至少是1个女儿的父母。他在英国英格兰奧可福爾瓦林福德被列为constable of wallingford castle。他去世于1336年3月25日在英国英格兰奧可福爾瓦林福德享年48岁。

nameMeanings.name: Bacon
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of American Family Names © Patrick Hanks 2003, 2006.
meanings: English (of Norman origin) and French: from the Norman French personal name Bacun, derived from the ancient Germanic name Bac(c)o, Bahho, based on the element bag ‘(to) fight, (to) dispute’. The name was relatively common among the Normans in the form Bacus, of which the oblique case was Bacon.

English and French: from Middle English, Old French bacun, bacon ‘bacon’ (a word of ancient Germanic origin, akin to Back 3), probably a metonymic occupational name for a preparer and seller of cured pork.

History: Gilles Bacon from Normandy, France, is documented in Quebec City, QC, in 1647. — Michael Bacon from England arrived in Dedham, MA, in 1640. Nathanial Bacon, from Stratton, Cornwall, arrived in Barnstaple, MA, in 1639. Another Nathaniel Bacon (1647–76), from Friston Hall, Suffolk, emigrated to VA and settled at Curl's Neck on the James river.

variants.name: Bagg
meanings: from Middle English bagge ‘bag, pack, bundle’, hence a metonymic occupational name for a maker of bags and sacks of various kinds, including wallets and purses. It might also have been used for a beggar, or for one who carried their belongings in a bag.
from the ancient Germanic personal name Bago (Bac(c)o, Bahho), from the root bag- ‘to fight’ (see Bacon 2).

variants.name: Back
meanings: English (Devon, Kent, Sussex, and Norfolk): from the Middle English personal name Bakke (Old English Bacca). It is of uncertain origin, but may have been a byname in the same sense as 3.
English: nickname from Middle English bakke ‘bat’, of uncertain application, perhaps a nickname for a person with poor eyesight, from the expression ‘blind as a bat’.

English: from Middle English bakke ‘back’ (Old English bæc), hence a nickname for someone with a hunched back or some other noticeable peculiarity of the back or spine, or a topographic name for someone who lived on a hill or ridge, or at the rear of a settlement.

nameMeanings.name: Edmund
nameMeanings.attribution: Dictionary of First Names © Patrick Hanks and Flavia Hodges 1990, 2003, 2006.
meanings: From an Old English personal name derived from ēad ‘prosperity, riches’ + mund ‘protector’. It was borne by several early royal and saintly figures, including a 9th-century king of East Anglia killed by invading Danes, allegedly for his adherence to Christianity. In the 16th and 17th centuries, there was a good deal of interchange between Edmund and Edward.

historicEvents.title: Jews Expelled From England
historicEvents.description: After years of anti-Semitic behavior, King Edward I expelled Jews from England in 1290.

historicEvents.title: Famine Strikes Europe
historicEvents.description: A famine caused by cold winters and wet summers across Europe caused millions to die due to starvation. Desperation from hunger caused high levels of crime, stampedes in bread lines in London, infanticide, and even cannibalism throughout Europe.

historicEvents.title: First War of Scottish Independence
historicEvents.description: The First War of Scottish Independence began after England invaded Scotland in 1296. The Treaty of Edinburgh was signed in 1328 which declared peace between the two kingdoms and brought the war to an end.

en.lifeSummary: When Edmund Bacon was born about 1289, in Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, England, his father, Adam Bacon, was 21 and his mother, Margery Felton, was 19. He married Joan Beaumont in 1311, in Oxfordshire, England. They were the parents of at least 1 daughter. His occupation is listed as constable of wallingford castle in Wallingford, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom. He died on 25 March 1336, in Wallingford, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom, at the age of 48.

fr.lifeSummary: Lorsque Edmund Bacon est né environ 1289, à Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, Angleterre, son père, Adam Bacon, avait 21 ans et sa mère, Margery Felton, avait 19 ans. Il a épousé Joan Beaumont en 1311, à Oxfordshire, Angleterre. Ils ont eu au moins 1 fille. Sa profession de son indiquée est constable of wallingford castle à Wallingford, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni. Il est décédé le 25 mars 1336, à Wallingford, Oxfordshire, Angleterre, Royaume-Uni, à l’âge de 48 ans.

de.lifeSummary: Als Edmund Bacon um 1289 in Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, England geboren wurde, war sein Vater Adam Bacon 21 und seine Mutter Margery Felton 19. Er hat 1311 in Oxfordshire, England Joan Beaumont geheiratet. Sie hatten mindestens 1 Tochter. sein Beruf ist als constable of wallingford castle in Wallingford, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom aufgeführt. Er starb am 25. März 1336 im Alter von 48 Jahren in Wallingford, Oxfordshire, England, United Kingdom.

it.lifeSummary: Quando Edmund Bacon è nato il circa 1289 nel Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, England, il suo padre, Adam Bacon, aveva 21 anni e il suo madre, Margery Felton, aveva 19 anni. Lui sposato Joan Beaumont nel 1311, nel Oxfordshire, England. Erano genitori di almeno 1 figlia. La sua occupazione è indicata come constable of wallingford castle nel Wallingford, Oxfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito. Morì il 25 marzo 1336, nel Wallingford, Oxfordshire, Inghilterra, Regno Unito, all'età di 48 anni.

ja.lifeSummary: Edmund Baconが約1289年にイギリスノーフォルクバコンスソルペで生まれた時，父Adam Baconは21歳，母Margery Feltonは19歳であった。1311年にイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアでJoan Beaumontと結婚した。彼らは少なくとも1人の娘の親であった。イギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアワリングフォードでの職業はconstable of wallingford castleと記録されている。彼は1336年3月25日にイギリスイギリスオクスフォードシュアイアワリングフォードで48歳で死去した。

ko.lifeSummary: Edmund Bacon(이)가 대략 1289년 영국 노르폴크 바콘스소에서 태어났을 때, 그의 아버지 Adam Bacon(은)는 21세였고, 그의 어머니 Margery Felton(은)는 19세였다. 그는 1311년 영국 스퍼드실르에서 Joan Beaumont(와)과 결혼했다. 그들은 적어도 한 명의 딸을 두었다. 그의 직업은 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 월링퍼드에서 constable of wallingford castle이었다. 그는 1336년 3월 25일 영국 잉글랜드 스퍼드실르 월링퍼드에서 48세의 나이로 사망했다.

pt.lifeSummary: Quando Edmund Bacon nasceu aproximadamente 1289, em Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra, seu pai, Adam Bacon, tinha 21 anos e sua mãe, Margery Felton, tinha 19 anos. Ele casou-se com Joan Beaumont em 1311, em Oxfordshire, Inglaterra. Eles tiveram pelo menos 1 filha. Sua profissão aparece como constable of wallingford castle em Wallingford, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom. Ele faleceu em 25 de março de 1336, em Wallingford, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, United Kingdom, com 48 anos.

ru.lifeSummary: Когда Edmund Bacon родился около 1289 г. в местности бакунстаурп, норфолк, Англия, его отцу по имени Adam Bacon было 21 год, а матери, которую звали Margery Felton, 19. Он женился на женщине по имени Joan Beaumont в 1311 г. в местности оксфордшир, Англия. У них была по крайней мере 1 дочь.  Он работал по профессии constable of wallingford castle в местности валлингфорд, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания. Он умер 25 марта 1336 г. в местности валлингфорд, оксфордшир, Англия, Великобритания в возрасте 48 лет.

es.lifeSummary: Cuando Edmund Bacon nació aproximadamente 1289, en Baconsthorpe, Norfolk, Inglaterra, su papá, Adam Bacon, tenía 21 años y su mamá, Margery Felton, tenía 19 años. Se casó con Joan Beaumont en 1311, en Oxfordshire, Inglaterra. Fueron padres de por lo menos 1 hija. Su ocupación registrada era constable of wallingford castle en Wallingford, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido. Murió el 25 de marzo de 1336, en Wallingford, Oxfordshire, Inglaterra, Reino Unido, a la edad de 48 años.
